Gameplay Description: Child flips cards to find matching animal pairs. Two matching cards stay revealed. Game ends when all pairs found.
Background/Visual Style: Animal sprites on card backs, random rotation ±3° per card, mismatch delay 0.8s
What It Teaches The Child: Short-term memory; visual recall; animal recognition
What We Can Learn About The Child: Memory capacity (how many pairs before mistakes); speed of recall; which animals are hardest to remember; pattern of mistakes (random vs systematic)
Cognitive Skills Required: Visual memory; spatial memory; concentration
Notes: Landscape orientation; cards have idle animations

Gameplay Description: Child drags puzzle pieces from left panel onto correct slots on right board. Pieces snap when placed correctly. Reference image visible at 25% alpha.
Background/Visual Style: Split screen: left=pieces, right=board. Warm sandy slot color (#C4B29F, 30% opacity), grid overlay
What It Teaches The Child: Spatial reasoning; shape matching; fine motor control; problem solving
What We Can Learn About The Child: Spatial ability (how quickly pieces are placed); trial-and-error patterns; fine motor precision (drag accuracy); persistence (time to complete)
Cognitive Skills Required: Spatial awareness; shape recognition; problem decomposition
Notes: Animal puzzle images from Art/Animals/Puzzle folder

Gameplay Description: Two modes: Brush Mode (ages 5+) = free drawing with 3 brush sizes + stickers + eraser. Area Fill Mode (ages 2-4) = tap regions to fill with color. Pre-built coloring pages or gallery upload.
Background/Visual Style: 12 palette colors, 1024×1024 canvas, outline overlay for coloring pages
Difficulty Level 1-3: Area Fill only (ages 2-4): tap color then tap shape region
Difficulty Level 4-6: Brush mode: free drawing with brush sizes 12/24/40px
Difficulty Level 7-10: Full brush mode + stickers + eraser + undo
Analytics Collected: RecordCorrect() on save drawing, fill count in area fill mode
What It Teaches The Child: Creativity; color recognition; fine motor skills; self-expression
What We Can Learn About The Child: Color preferences (which colors used most); motor control (stroke quality); creativity level (variety of marks); attention span (time spent)
Cognitive Skills Required: Color recognition; spatial awareness; creative thinking
Notes: Excluded from Journey mode; mode configurable in parent dashboard

Gameplay Description: Child drags through numbered dots (1→2→3→...) to draw a constellation-style shape. Shape closes when returning to dot 1. Night sky aesthetic.
Background/Visual Style: Night sky gradient, warm constellation lines (#FFF5BF), glowing star-like dots
Difficulty Level 1-3: Tier 0: Simple shapes (triangle, square, flower) — 3-5 dots
Difficulty Level 4-6: Tier 1: Medium shapes (star, heart, house) — 5-8 dots
Difficulty Level 7-10: Tier 2: Complex shapes — 8-10 dots, animal silhouettes
What It Teaches The Child: Sequential ordering; number sequencing; shape recognition; fine motor control
What We Can Learn About The Child: Number familiarity (speed of finding next number); motor control (path accuracy); shape recognition ability
Cognitive Skills Required: Sequential thinking; number recognition; shape awareness
Notes: Animal silhouettes auto-generated from sprites; 8 fallback shapes

Gameplay Description: 4 animal silhouettes on top, 4 draggable colored animals on bottom. Child drags each animal to its matching shadow. One-to-one matching.
Background/Visual Style: Silhouettes in dark color (#0F0F2E, 90% opacity), proximity hints pulse when animal dragged nearby
What It Teaches The Child: Shape recognition; animal identification; visual matching
What We Can Learn About The Child: Shape matching ability; visual discrimination; motor precision; speed of recognition
Cognitive Skills Required: Shape matching; visual discrimination; animal knowledge
Notes: hasSubItems=true required for Journey mode; silhouettes use custom shader

Gameplay Description: 12 animals placed across 3 depth zones in scenic world. Child must find 3-6 target animals. Strict grid prevents overlap. Hints after 3.5s inactivity.
Background/Visual Style: Landscape world with sky gradient, ground, grass, clouds, decorations (trees, houses). 5 sky + 5 ground color variations.
Difficulty Level 1-3: Easy tier: Large animals (Elephant, Giraffe, Horse, Cow, Lion)
Difficulty Level 4-6: Medium tier: Medium animals (Dog, Cat, Sheep, Monkey, Donkey, Bear, Zebra)
Difficulty Level 7-10: Hard tier: Small animals (Chicken, Duck, Bird, Fish, Frog, Snake, Turtle)
Analytics Collected: SetCustom("targetsFound"), plays correct effect per find
What It Teaches The Child: Visual searching; patience; animal recognition; attention to detail
What We Can Learn About The Child: Visual scanning ability; patience level; attention to detail; recognition speed; which animals are hardest to find
Cognitive Skills Required: Visual scanning; sustained attention; categorization
Notes: hasSubItems=true; animal scale varies 0.88-1.3x per species

Gameplay Description: Shows target number at top, 4 animal-group tiles at bottom. Child taps tile whose animal count matches target. 5 rounds total.
Background/Visual Style: 4 rounded-rect tiles with animals, pop-in animation, inactivity hints
What It Teaches The Child: Counting; number recognition; quantity comparison
What We Can Learn About The Child: Counting accuracy; number recognition speed; which quantities are hardest; comparison ability
Cognitive Skills Required: Counting; number comparison; visual estimation

Gameplay Description: Tap colors from palette (R/B/Y/W) to fill containers. Containers pour into mixing vessel. Liquid swirls and settles to result color. 5 combinations: R+Y=Orange, B+Y=Green, R+B=Purple, R+W=Pink, B+W=LightBlue.
Background/Visual Style: Interactive containers with fill levels, glow effects, target color circle
Analytics Collected: RecordCorrect() on successful mix, target pulses for hint
What It Teaches The Child: Color theory; cause-and-effect; observation; prediction
What We Can Learn About The Child: Understanding of color relationships; prediction ability; experimental thinking
Cognitive Skills Required: Color knowledge; cause-effect reasoning; prediction

Gameplay Description: Child drags ball across wooden board navigating obstacles and rotating blockers. Physics-based with inertia and bounce. Timed past rotating barriers.
Background/Visual Style: Wooden board with frame, ball shadow, rotating blockers. Cream table (#E8DFCF), wood frame (#C29E6B)
What It Teaches The Child: Physics understanding; timing; spatial navigation; patience
What We Can Learn About The Child: Motor control precision; timing ability; spatial planning; persistence with physics challenges
Cognitive Skills Required: Spatial reasoning; timing; physics intuition

Gameplay Description: Reference tower shown on left, silhouette slots on right. Child drags colored bricks from palette to match reference. Support graph enforces build order (bottom-up).
Background/Visual Style: Split screen: left=reference (colored), right=build area (silhouettes), bottom=palette. Ground platform.
Analytics Collected: SetCustom per brick, wrong attempt tracking
What It Teaches The Child: Spatial reasoning; color/pattern matching; logical sequencing; planning
What We Can Learn About The Child: Pattern matching ability; sequencing logic; color recognition; planning approach (bottom-up vs random attempts)
Cognitive Skills Required: Pattern matching; sequential logic; color recognition
Notes: Support graph prevents placing upper bricks before lower ones

Gameplay Description: Spot It/Dobble style. Two circular cards show stickers; exactly one matches on both. Child taps the shared sticker. Stickers per card increases over rounds.
Background/Visual Style: Two circular cards, stickers in spiral/ring layout, random rotation ±15° per sticker
Analytics Collected: RecordCorrect() per tap, manual confetti every 3 rounds
What It Teaches The Child: Visual scanning; speed; attention to detail; comparison
What We Can Learn About The Child: Visual processing speed; attention to detail; search strategy (systematic vs random); performance under increasing complexity
Cognitive Skills Required: Visual scanning; rapid comparison; sustained attention
Notes: Stickers loaded from all sprite sheets in Art/Stickers/

Gameplay Description: Bird flies forward, child taps to flap upward. Pipes scroll from right. Hit pipe or ground = game over. Best score tracked. Pre-spawned pipes visible before first tap.
Background/Visual Style: Wooden pipes, ground at 16%, bird tilt follows velocity (±30°)
Difficulty Level 1-3: Fixed: gravity -1200; flap 420; pipe speed 250; gap 520px
What It Teaches The Child: Timing; reflexes; persistence; frustration tolerance
What We Can Learn About The Child: Reaction time; persistence (retry count); frustration handling; timing precision
Cognitive Skills Required: Timing; anticipation; persistence

Gameplay Description: 4 colored buttons light up in sequence. Child repeats by tapping in same order. Each successful round adds one color. Endless with best round tracked.
Background/Visual Style: Wooden toy board, 4 large buttons (red/yellow/blue/green) with glow overlays, smooth flash animation
Analytics Collected: Endless best round in profile.progress, bestScore
What It Teaches The Child: Sequential memory; pattern recognition; attention; auditory-visual association
What We Can Learn About The Child: Working memory capacity; sequential recall length; speed of learning; attention span; error patterns
Cognitive Skills Required: Sequential memory; pattern recognition; sustained attention
Notes: Step duration adjusted by speed multiplier

Gameplay Description: Source grid (left) shows filled pattern. Player grid (right) empty. Child taps cells to toggle ON/OFF. Auto-wins when pattern matches. Hints after 5s inactivity.
Background/Visual Style: Two grids side-by-side, cells colored with profile avatar color
What It Teaches The Child: Visual matching; pattern replication; spatial reasoning; attention to detail
What We Can Learn About The Child: Pattern recognition accuracy; spatial memory; attention to detail; error rate; strategy (row-by-row vs random)
Cognitive Skills Required: Pattern matching; spatial memory; systematic approach

Gameplay Description: Shows animal image + Hebrew word with missing first letter + 3 answer buttons. Child taps correct first letter. Word announced after 0.5s. 5 rounds.
Background/Visual Style: Split screen: right=animal image, left=word tiles (RTL). 3 answer buttons, animated animals, replay button
What It Teaches The Child: Hebrew alphabet; phonemic awareness; word recognition; letter-sound correspondence
What We Can Learn About The Child: Letter knowledge (which letters known/unknown); phonemic awareness level; word length comfort; common confusion pairs
Cognitive Skills Required: Letter recognition; phonemic awareness; word reading

Gameplay Description: Grid of numbered tiles. Child taps 1→2→3→...→N following orthogonal adjacent path. Green path line drawn. Auto-wins on completion. Inactivity hints.
Background/Visual Style: Grid centered, start cell highlighted, path lines with glow (green #42A038, 80% opacity), staggered bounce-in
What It Teaches The Child: Number sequencing; path planning; counting; spatial navigation
What We Can Learn About The Child: Number fluency (speed); path planning ability; counting accuracy; spatial reasoning; error patterns
Cognitive Skills Required: Number sequencing; spatial planning; systematic search

Gameplay Description: 4 animals in horizontal row — 3 identical, 1 different. Child taps the odd one. 5 rounds total.
Background/Visual Style: 4 rounded-rect cards in row, animals with idle animations, pop-in stagger animation
What It Teaches The Child: Visual discrimination; same/different concept; animal recognition; attention
What We Can Learn About The Child: Visual discrimination ability; processing speed; attention to detail; which animal pairs are most confused
Cognitive Skills Required: Visual discrimination; comparison; categorization

Gameplay Description: Train of wagons with numbers scrolls in. Some wagons empty (gray). Child drags missing numbers from palette into correct empty wagons. Train exits on completion.
Background/Visual Style: Train slides in from left, blue filled wagons, gray empty wagons, number palette at bottom
Analytics Collected: RecordCorrect() per placement, SetCustom("wagonCount"), SetCustom("missingCount"), SetCustom("startNumber")
What It Teaches The Child: Number sequencing; counting; gap-filling; number line concepts
What We Can Learn About The Child: Number fluency; gap-filling ability; number line understanding; which number ranges are strongest/weakest
Cognitive Skills Required: Number sequencing; gap identification; counting

Gameplay Description: Train with Hebrew letters (א-ת). Some wagons empty. Child drags missing letters from palette. RTL layout (first letter rightmost). Endless rounds.
Background/Visual Style: Similar to Number Train, purple scheme (#D1C4E9), Hebrew letters, RTL reading order
What It Teaches The Child: Hebrew alphabet sequence; letter recognition; RTL reading
What We Can Learn About The Child: Alphabet knowledge (which sections known); letter recognition speed; RTL reading fluency; common letter confusions
Cognitive Skills Required: Letter sequencing; alphabet knowledge; RTL awareness

Gameplay Description: Reference pattern on left (connected dots). Right side: interactive dot grid. Child drags to connect dots in same sequence. Can start from either end. Auto-wins on match.
Background/Visual Style: Split screen, dots in profile avatar color, lines with glow effect, live drag line visible
What It Teaches The Child: Path sequencing; directional awareness; fine motor control; pattern replication
What We Can Learn About The Child: Directional awareness; sequential planning; motor control precision; pattern complexity handling
Cognitive Skills Required: Sequential logic; directional thinking; path planning

Gameplay Description: Drag clothes into washing machine, fruits into basket. All items must be sorted correctly. Spiral arrangement pattern for spawning.
Background/Visual Style: Split screen: left=washing machine, right=basket, items in middle area
What It Teaches The Child: Categorization; sorting; object recognition; classification
What We Can Learn About The Child: Classification ability; speed of categorization; error patterns (which items misclassified); conceptual understanding of categories
Cognitive Skills Required: Classification; sorting; category knowledge

Gameplay Description: Elroey in boat above water. Speech bubble shows target fish shape. Child taps matching fish swimming below. 5 catches to win. Fishing line animation.
Background/Visual Style: Layered sea (sky, clouds, water bands, sand). Elroey with boat, rod tip anchor. 8 shape-based fish.
What It Teaches The Child: Shape matching; visual tracking; target identification; patience
What We Can Learn About The Child: Shape recognition accuracy; visual tracking ability; confusion pairs (which shapes are mixed up); reaction speed
Cognitive Skills Required: Shape recognition; visual tracking; selective attention

Gameplay Description: Shows 1-12 animated animals on screen. Child taps correct count from 3 answer buttons. Counting celebration plays number sounds and highlights each animal.
Background/Visual Style: Animals pop in staggered, predefined positions per count, large number during celebration with color coding
What It Teaches The Child: Counting; number recognition; one-to-one correspondence; subitizing
What We Can Learn About The Child: Counting fluency; subitizing ability (instant recognition vs counting); number range comfort; which quantities cause errors
Cognitive Skills Required: Counting; number recognition; estimation
